requirement: Submitting new work from the UI isn't working. Once the Start Work buttons is pressed work should begin.

feature_slices.description: Implement the Start Work button interaction lifecycle: immediate loading/disabled state, UUID-based idempotency key generation, API submission with proper error boundaries, and a polling loop to track job status and update UI. Includes rollback logic on failure and clear error messaging.
acceptance: Button disables immediately on click with a visible spinner/loading state
Idempotency key (UUIDv4) is generated and sent in header on submission request
Polling loop fetches job status and updates UI (e.g., progress, success state)
Network errors or 4xx/5xx responses display user-friendly error banners
Rapid clicking does not result in duplicate API calls or state corruption
UI rolls back loading state and re-enables button on submission failure

feature_slices.description: Implement the POST /work/submit endpoint with input validation, idempotency check, job record creation, and async queue dispatch. Implement GET /jobs/:id endpoint to retrieve job status. Returns 202 Accepted immediately upon successful queueing. Includes rate limiting per user/session.
acceptance: POST /work/submit validates payload and returns 400 for invalid input
Submission accepts X-Idempotency-Key header and rejects duplicates with 409
Endpoint returns 202 Accepted with job ID immediately after queue dispatch
GET /jobs/:id returns current job status and metadata
Rate limiting prevents excessive submission requests from a single user
Input sanitization prevents injection attacks in work parameters

feature_slices.description: Configure the background task queue infrastructure: set up broker/queue connection, define the work execution task function, and ensure the worker process is configured to execute tasks asynchronously. Decouples work execution from the request thread to prevent blocking and timeouts.
acceptance: Task queue broker connection is configured and verified
Work execution task is defined and registered with the queue system
Worker process runs asynchronously without blocking HTTP request threads
Task execution handles failures gracefully and updates job status
Queue connection pooling is configured for concurrent submission handling

- Frontend UI with a "Start Work" button
- Backend API to handle work submission
